title: Ctenopteris sericeolanata
taxonomy: Ctenopteris sericeolanata is classified as a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It is native to New Zealand. The exact classification of Ctenopteris sericeolanata is Ctenopteris sericeolanata (C. Chr.) Holttum. The genus Ctenopteris is a group of ferns that are found in New Zealand and the South Pacific. The family Dryopteridaceae is a large and diverse group of ferns with over 2,000 species worldwide.

intro: Ctenopteris sericeolanata, also known as the silky maidenhair fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. The silky maidenhair fern has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation and antioxidant activity, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Ctenopteris sericeolanata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and polysaccharides, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties.
